# RFC-0004: Benchmark artifact comparison semantics

> **Version:** 0.1.0 | **Status:** normative | **Phase:** test

---

## 1. Summary

### [RFC-0004:C-SUMMARY] Summary (Informative) <a id="rfc-0004c-summary"></a>

This RFC defines how `agent-skill-bench` compares two benchmark artifact sets to surface regressions and improvements.

**Scope:** comparison inputs, run matching semantics, aggregate delta semantics, and required comparison outputs.

**Rationale:** [RFC-0003](../rfc/RFC-0003.md) makes normalized artifacts stable enough to consume, but users still need a first-class way to compare two artifact sets without writing ad hoc scripts around report output.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---

## 2. Specification

### [RFC-0004:C-COMPARISON-INPUTS] Comparison inputs (Normative) <a id="rfc-0004c-comparison-inputs"></a>

A comparison operation MUST consume exactly two normalized artifact sets, one treated as the baseline set and one treated as the candidate set.
Both artifact sets MUST be interpreted through the stable artifact semantics defined by [RFC-0003](../rfc/RFC-0003.md) rather than through adapter-specific metadata.
A comparison implementation MUST reject inputs that cannot be read as normalized artifact sets.
A comparison implementation MUST preserve the distinction between aggregate summaries derived from each set and deltas computed between those summaries.

**Rationale:** users need a comparison command that operates on stable artifacts, not on whatever runtime-specific evidence happened to be emitted during execution. Keeping baseline summaries separate from computed deltas avoids conflating raw measurements with regression interpretation.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0004:C-RUN-MATCHING] Run matching and deltas (Normative) <a id="rfc-0004c-run-matching"></a>

A comparison implementation MUST match runs across the baseline and candidate sets by benchmark identity, meaning the suite and case represented by each artifact.
A comparison implementation MUST treat unmatched runs as explicit additions or removals rather than silently dropping them.
If either input set contains multiple artifacts for the same benchmark identity, the comparison implementation MUST fail clearly or otherwise report the ambiguity explicitly; it MUST NOT choose one arbitrarily.
For matched runs, the comparison implementation MUST be able to surface changes in deterministic pass/fail status, candidate runtime outcome, judge runtime outcome when present, and deterministic failure-mode codes.

**Rationale:** regression comparison is only meaningful when both sides refer to the same benchmark case. Silent duplicate resolution or silent dropping of unmatched runs would make diffs look cleaner while hiding the very regressions or coverage gaps the user is trying to inspect.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0004:C-COMPARISON-OUTPUT] Comparison output semantics (Normative) <a id="rfc-0004c-comparison-output"></a>

A comparison output MUST report aggregate deltas for deterministic pass rate between the baseline and candidate sets.
A comparison output MUST report aggregate deltas for runtime outcome distributions and deterministic failure-mode distributions between the baseline and candidate sets.
A comparison output SHOULD include grouped deltas for at least suite and mode so regressions can be localized.
A comparison output MUST report unmatched benchmark identities separately from matched-run deltas.
The comparison contract MAY evolve its concrete layout over time, but it MUST preserve those comparison semantics independently of any particular CLI formatting choice.

**Rationale:** users need to see not only whether overall pass rate moved, but also whether the change came from runtime failures, contract failures, or specific benchmark slices. Treating unmatched runs separately keeps aggregate deltas honest when the compared sets cover different case populations.

*Since: v0.1.0*
